title: AP 物理C力学 2025年样题3
description: AP AP 物理C力学 2025年样题3

scoring.note: Scoring unavailable until answer keys are imported.

questions.prompt:
A satellite of mass m is in orbit around a spherical asteroid of mass M and radius R . The asteroid-satellite system has a gravitational potential energy Ug=−5GMm7R.

What is the altitude of the satellite above the surface of the asteroid?

options.content: 27R
25R
57R
75R

questions.explanation: Answer key not available yet for this imported exam.

questions.prompt:
A train car carrying an 800kg stone is moving to the right with constant speed v T = 20 m/s . The stone is at rest relative to the train car. The coefficients of static and kinetic friction for the stone on the train car’s surface are 0.30 and 0.15, respectively.

What is the magnitude of the force of friction exerted on the stone by the train car?

options.content: Zero
1.2×103N
2.4×103N
16×103N

questions.explanation: Answer key not available yet for this imported exam.

questions.prompt:
A cart moves forward along a linear track that may or may not have varying heights above the ground. Starting at time t = 0 , the horizontal position of the cart along the track is marked at regular intervals of time, as shown in Figure 1. The only forces that are exerted on the cart are from the track and Earth. Friction between the cart and the track is negligible.

Which of the following figures depict a track that could result in the motion indicated in Figure 1 ?
questions.explanation: Answer key not available yet for this imported exam.

questions.prompt:
Two identical blocks, A and B, are sliding across a horizontal surface. Friction between the surface and the blocks is negligible. Block A is initially traveling to the right with speed v0. Block B is initially traveling to the left with speed 2v0, as shown. At time t=t1, the blocks undergo an elastic collision.

Which of the following pairs of graphs could represent the kinetic energies KA and KB of blocks A and B, respectively, as functions of time?
questions.explanation: Answer key not available yet for this imported exam.

questions.prompt:
In a laboratory demonstration, a constant force is exerted on a uniform disk tangent to the disk’s edge. The disk rotates about an axle through its center with negligible friction.

Which of the following changes to the demonstration would increase the angular acceleration of the disk?

options.content: Replacing the original disk with a ring that has the same mass and radius as the disk.
Replacing the original disk with a disk that has the same mass and radius but has a density that decreases radially from its center.
Replacing the original disk with a disk that has twice the radius but the same mass as the original disk.
Exerting the force perpendicular to the edge of the disk rather than tangent to the edge.

questions.explanation: Answer key not available yet for this imported exam.

questions.prompt:
At time t=0, a rigid wheel begins to roll across a surface without slipping. The linear position x of the wheel’s center of mass is given by x(t)=At3, where A is a positive constant. The angular acceleration α of the wheel is given by α(t)=2(t).

What is the radius of the wheel?

options.content: 13A
12A
2A
3A

questions.prompt:
Two thin, spherical shells each have the same mass M but different radii of R and 3R The shells are concentric with each other. Points P and Q are located distances 2R and 4R, respectively, from the centers of the shells. The gravitational field strength at points P and Q is gP and gQ, respectively.

What is the ratio g P g Q ?

options.content: 12
11
21
41

questions.prompt:
A block of mass m is attached to a string that wraps many times around a pulley of radius R as shown. The block is released and falls downward, resulting in an angular velocity ω of the pulley as a function of time t that is modeled by ω=βt, where β is a positive constant. The string does not slip on the pulley.

At time t 1 , what is the vertical displacement of the block?

options.content: β2Rt12
β2t12
βR
β

questions.prompt:
A block of mass m is initially at rest on a horizontal table at position x = 0 . The block is then pulled by a variable horizontal force. The magnitude of the net force exerted on the block is described by F=Ax3, where A is a positive constant.

After moving a distance x 1 , what is the speed of the block?

options.content: 6Amx1
A2mx12
2Amx12
A4mx14

questions.prompt:
A 500kg rocket is initially at rest on the ground, where gravitational potential energy of the rocket-Earth system is taken to be zero. At time t = 0 , the engines turn on, resulting in a constant net vertical force of 4,000N being exerted on the rocket. The engines turn off at t=5.0s.

What is the total mechanical energy of the rocket-Earth system at t = 5.0 s ?

options.content: 4.0×105J
5.2×105J
9.0×105J
14.0×105J

questions.prompt:
An ideal spring of length L is attached to a ceiling and hangs vertically. A block is attached to the bottom of the spring, pulled downward, and released. The length of the spring as the block oscillates varies between 1.00m and 1.10m. The maximum acceleration of the block is measured to be 2.00 m/s 2 .

Which of the following is most nearly the length L of the spring?

options.content: 0.25m
0.80m
1.00m
1.05m

questions.prompt:
A block is connected by a string to a sphere. The block is on a ramp inclined at an angle of 30 ° to the horizontal, as shown. The string passes over a pulley of negligible mass and friction, and the sphere hangs freely. The block accelerates down the ramp with negligible friction.

Which of the following statements is true?

options.content: The mass of the sphere is greater than the mass of the block.
The mass of the sphere is equal to the mass of the block.
The weight of the sphere is greater than the component of the block’s weight along the ramp.
The weight of the sphere is less than the component of the block’s weight along the ramp.

questions.prompt:
A 0.30kg cart is initially at rest on a track. A student uses a force sensor and exerts a horizontal force on the cart. The force F exerted by the sensor on the cart as a function of time t is shown in the graph.

What is the approximate change in the momentum of the cart during the time interval shown?

options.content: 2.0kg·m/s
9.0kg·m/s
13kg·m/s
25kg·m/s

questions.prompt:
A block of mass m=0.20kg is attached to a spring of spring constant k = 30 N/m and placed on a horizontal surface. The block is pulled to the right to a position x=0.12m relative to equilibrium, as shown, and then released. Friction between the block and surface is negligible.

As the block passes through the position x = 0.04 m , what is the kinetic energy of the block?

options.content: 0.024J
0.096J
0.192J
0.216J

questions.prompt:
Two projectiles are launched from a level surface. Projectile 1 has an initial launch velocity v → 0 = A i ^ + A j ^ , where A is a positive constant, as shown. Projectile 2 is launched with a different initial velocity such that its horizontal range and time of flight is four times greater than that of Projectile 1.

Which vector could represent the initial velocity of Projectile 2 ?

questions.prompt:
In Scenario 1, two blocks, A and B, are connected by a string that passes over an ideal pulley, as shown. The mass of blocks A and B is mA and mB, respectively, where mA>mB. The two blocks are released from rest and allowed to accelerate.

While the blocks are accelerating in Scenario 1, which of the following forces must have a smaller magnitude than the tension in the string?

options.content: Neither mAg nor mBg
Only mAg
Only mBg
Both mAg and mBg

questions.explanation: Answer key not available yet for this imported exam.

questions.prompt:
In Scenario 1, two blocks, A and B, are connected by a string that passes over an ideal pulley, as shown. The mass of blocks A and B is mA and mB, respectively, where mA>mB. The two blocks are released from rest and allowed to accelerate.

In Scenario 2, Block A is replaced with Block C, as shown. The mass of block C is mC, where mC>mA. The tension in the string in Scenarios 1 and 2 is T1 and T2, respectively. Which of the following correctly compares T1 and T2, and includes a correct justification for that comparison?

options.content: T1>T2. The net force exerted on the larger mass in Scenario 1 is greater than that in Scenario 2.
T1>T2. The ratio between the smaller and larger mass in Scenario 1 is greater than that in Scenario 2.
T1<T2. The tension required in the string to keep the larger mass in equilibrium in Scenario 2 is greater than that in Scenario 1.
T1<T2. The tension in the string is greater because the acceleration of Block B is greater in Scenario 2 than in Scenario 1.

questions.prompt:
A block of mass m is pulled by a rope along a horizontal surface with negligible friction. For times t > 0 , the magnitude of the acceleration of the object is given by the equation a=Ae−kt, where A and k are positive constants.

Which of the following expressions would evaluate to the impulse exerted on the block by the rope during the time interval 0 < t < t 1 ?

options.content: A∫0t1e−ktdt
mA∫0t1e−ktdt
Addt[e−kt]t1
mAddt[e−kt]t1

questions.prompt:
A block of mass m is pulled on a horizontal surface by a string attached to a motor as seen in the figure. Friction between the block and surface is negligible. For times, t such that 0<t<t1, the motor exerts a time-varying force on the string, such that the instantaneous power P delivered to the block by the motor is modeled by P=At+B, where A and B are positive constants.

At time t 1 , how much work has the motor done on the block?

options.content: At1t1+B
A(t1+B)2
Aln(t1+B)
Aln(t1+BB)

questions.prompt:
Blocks A and B, of mass mA and mB, respectively, are both initially at rest on a horizontal floor. The net force Fnet on Blocks A and B varies as a function of position x, which is shown in Graphs A and B respectively. When Blocks A and B are at x=4m, their respective velocities are vA and vB.

Which of the following statements comparing vA to vB, and comparing mA to m B must be true?

options.content: If vA=vB, then mA=mB.
If vA=vB, then mA>mB.
If vA>vB, then mA>mB.
If vA>vB, then mA<mB.

questions.prompt:
A can of beans of mass m is attached to the end of a vertical spring of spring constant k that is secured to the ceiling. At time t=0, the can is released from a position at which the spring is neither stretched or compressed and allowed to fall. At time t 1 , the can has moved a downward distance d=mgk from its initial position, and the acceleration of the can is a1.

Which of the following statements correctly describes a 1 , and provides a correct justification?

options.content: a1=0, because the can is at the equilibrium position of the can-spring-Earth system.
a1=0, because the velocity of the can is downwards and the force of the spring on the can is upwards.
a1=maximum, because the speed of the can is at its maximum.
a1=maximum, because the displacement of the can and the force of gravity on the can are in the same direction.

questions.prompt: The position x of an object changes with respect to time t is modeled by x(t)=αt3−βt, where α and β are positive constants and t≥0. What is the time or times at which the object momentarily comes to rest?

options.content: t=0 only
t=β3α only
t=0 and t=βα
t=0 and t=2βα

questions.prompt:
Runners P and Q are running in the same direction. At position x=0, runners P and Q have speeds vP and vQ respectively, as shown. While running, the acceleration of Runner P is aP, where aP>0, and the acceleration of Runner Q is 2 a P . When each runner reaches position x=xf, the runners have the same speed.

Which of the following correctly compares v P and vQ?

options.content: vP>vQ
vP<vQ
(vP=vQ)>0
vP=vQ=0

questions.prompt:

A spring with spring constant k is fixed to a point on the table and perpendicularly to one end of the rod. The rod is displaced a small angle θ from equilibrium and released, as shown in the Top View figure. The rod oscillates in simple harmonic motion with period T .

What is the mass of the rod?

34kT2π2
32kT2π2
3kT2π2

questions.prompt:
A small toy car travels with negligible friction around a track with vertical circular loop. Point P is located at a position along the track after the car has passed by the top of the loop, as shown.

When the car is at Point P, which force, or forces, has a component that results in the centripetal and tangential accelerations of the car?
Forces That Cause Centripetal Acceleration Forces That Cause Tangential Acceleration
A Normal force only None
B Normal force only Force of gravity
C Normal force and the force of gravity None
D Normal force and the force of gravity Force of gravity

options.content: A
B
C
D

Considering counterclockwise to be the direction of positive torque, which disk will have the greatest angular speed at time t f ?

The friction between the disk and the brake slows the rotation of the disk, which comes to a stop in two full rotations.

questions.prompt:
A small spacecraft approaches the midpoint between two large asteroids that are far from any other objects. There is zero thrust exerted on the spacecraft from the spacecraft’s engines. The mass of each asteroid is much greater than that of the spacecraft and the distance between the asteroids remains constant.

As the spacecraft approaches, which of the following describes the change in gravitational potential energy Δ U g of the asteroids-spacecraft system, and includes a correct justification?

options.content: ΔUg decreases because the gravitational force of each asteroid does negative work on the spacecraft.
ΔUg decreases because the gravitational force of each asteroid does positive work on the spacecraft.
ΔUg increases because the gravitational force of each asteroid does positive work on the spacecraft.
ΔUg increases because the gravitational force of each asteroid does negative work on the spacecraft.

questions.prompt:
The position x as a function of time t for an object moving along the x -axis is shown in the figure. Positive values of position correspond to locations to the right of the origin.

Which of the following graphs could represent the acceleration a of the object as a function of t , and includes a correct justification?
a vs. t graph Justification
A The object moves towards the origin and then away from the origin.
B While moving to the right, the speed of the object increases and then decreases.
C While moving to the left the object changes speed, and then changes speed while moving to the right.
D While moving to the right, the speed of the object decreases and then increases.

options.content: A
B
C
D
